summary:
I have found and fixed the issue. The `process_file` function in `src/main.rs` was only reading a maximum of `BUFFER_SIZE` (64KB) of data because it used a single `read` call, silently truncating files larger than 64KB.

I updated `process_file` to read the input file in a `loop`, chunking the data in 64KB segments, transforming them, and writing them iteratively until the end of the file is reached.

I verified the fix by running `./test.sh` which now successfully copies a 100KB file without data loss and passes with `PASS: File processed correctly, no data loss`.
